name: nettrace-agentix
description: AI-powered network trace analysis. Parses pcap/pcapng captures with tshark and provides intelligent, agentic diagnosis via GitHub Copilot.

chatParticipants.name: nettrace
chatParticipants.description: Analyze the network capture open in the viewer. Just ask a question.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-getStreamDetail
languageModelTools.description: Retrieves full packet-level detail for a specific TCP stream index from a capture file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieves full packet-level detail (timestamps, flags, seq/ack numbers, payload) for a specific TCP stream index from a network capture file. Use this to inspect individual connections in depth.

streamIndex.description: The TCP stream index to retrieve

captureFile.description: Optional: specific capture file path. Uses active capture if omitted.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-getPacketRange
languageModelTools.description: Fetches packets by frame number range for paginating through large captures
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetches packets within a frame number range from a capture file. Use this to page through sections of a large capture, inspect packets around a specific event, or view packets in a time window. Returns up to 500 packets per call. Optionally apply a Wireshark display filter to narrow results within the range.

endFrame.description: Last frame number to fetch (inclusive, clamped to 500 frames max)

filter.description: Optional Wireshark display filter to apply within the range (e.g., 'tcp', 'dns')

captureFile.description: Optional: specific capture file path. Uses active capture if omitted.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-getExpertInfo
languageModelTools.description: Retrieves Wireshark expert information (errors, warnings, notes) from the capture
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieves Wireshark expert information (errors, warnings, notes, chats) from the capture. Use this to find protocol-level issues like retransmissions, malformed packets, or connection problems. Optionally filter by severity.

severity.description: Filter by severity level. Defaults to 'all'.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-applyFilter
languageModelTools.description: Applies a Wireshark display filter and returns matching packets
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Applies a Wireshark display filter expression to a capture file and returns matching packets. Use any valid Wireshark display filter syntax (e.g., 'tcp.analysis.retransmission', 'dns', 'http.response.code == 500'). Returns up to maxPackets matching packets. By default, also pushes the filter to the capture viewer panel so the user sees the filtered view.

maxPackets.description: Maximum number of packets to return. Defaults to 100.

applyToPanel.description: If true (default), also pushes the filter to the Capture Viewer panel so the user sees filtered packets. Set to false if you only need data for analysis without changing the user's view.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-getConversations
languageModelTools.description: Lists all TCP/UDP conversations with statistics (bytes, packets, duration)
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all TCP, UDP, or IP conversations in a capture with statistics including byte counts, packet counts, duration, and anomaly indicators. Use this to get an overview of all network connections and identify suspicious ones.

protocol.description: Protocol layer for conversations. Defaults to 'tcp'.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-followStream
languageModelTools.description: Reconstructs and returns the application-layer payload of a TCP stream (like Wireshark Follow Stream)
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reconstructs and returns the application-layer payload data of a TCP stream, similar to Wireshark's 'Follow TCP Stream' feature. Shows the actual data exchanged between client and server in ascii, hex, or raw format. Use this to inspect HTTP requests/responses, TLS handshakes, or other application-layer protocols.

streamIndex.description: The TCP stream index to follow

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-compareCaptures
languageModelTools.description: Compares packets between matched client and server captures to find missing, delayed, or modified packets
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Compares network captures taken at client-side and server-side to find discrepancies: missing packets, stream count differences, and differing expert info. Use this when the user has simultaneous captures from both ends of a connection to identify where packets are being lost or modified in transit.

filterExpression.description: Optional display filter to narrow comparison

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-setDisplayFilter
languageModelTools.description: Pushes a Wireshark display filter to the active Capture Viewer panel, updating the packet display in real-time
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Pushes a Wireshark display filter into the active Capture Viewer panel (the UI the user sees). This UPDATES THE USER'S VIEW — packets shown in the viewer will be filtered to match. Use this when the user asks to filter, show, or focus on specific traffic (e.g., 'show me DNS traffic', 'filter to port 443'). Pass an empty string to clear the filter. This does NOT return data to you — use nettrace-applyFilter to get filtered packet data for your analysis.

filter.description: Wireshark display filter expression to apply to the viewer (e.g., 'dns', 'tcp.port == 443', 'ip.addr == 10.0.0.1'). Pass empty string to clear.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-runTshark
languageModelTools.description: Executes a custom tshark command with arbitrary read-only arguments against a capture file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs a custom tshark command against the active capture file. You can use any tshark read-only arguments: display filters (-Y), field extraction (-T fields -e), statistics (-z), protocol decoding, etc. The capture file is automatically provided via -r. DO NOT include -r in your args. Write operations (-w) are blocked for safety. Use this for specialized analysis not covered by other tools, like extracting specific protocol fields, running IO statistics, computing round-trip times, or any tshark capability. If applyToPanel is true, any -Y display filter in your args will also be pushed to the capture viewer panel.

args.description: tshark arguments (excluding -r and the capture file). Example: '-Y dns -T fields -e dns.qry.name -e dns.qry.type -e dns.flags.rcode'

captureFile.description: Optional: specific capture file path. Uses active capture if omitted.

applyToPanel.description: If true and args contain a -Y filter, also push that filter to the capture viewer panel. Defaults to false.

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-createAgent
languageModelTools.description: Creates a new analysis agent JSON file in the .nettrace/agents/ folder
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new analysis agent with a custom persona, tools, and filters. Use this when a user asks to create a specialized agent (e.g., 'create an agent for HTTP performance'). The agent will appear in the sidebar and can be activated for focused analysis. Agents change HOW the AI analyzes — they set the persona, select which tools to use, and optionally pre-filter traffic.

description.description: Short description of what this agent specializes in

systemPrompt.description: The full system prompt / persona instructions for this agent

displayFilter.description: Optional Wireshark display filter to pre-filter traffic (e.g., 'http', 'tcp.port == 443')

tools.description: Tool names this agent can use. Available: nettrace-getStreamDetail, nettrace-getPacketRange, nettrace-applyFilter, nettrace-getConversations, nettrace-followStream, nettrace-getExpertInfo, nettrace-compareCaptures, nettrace-runTshark, nettrace-setDisplayFilter

followups.description: Suggested follow-up prompts shown after analysis

languageModelTools.name: nettrace-createKnowledge
languageModelTools.description: Creates a new knowledge markdown file in .nettrace/knowledge/ to teach the AI about specific patterns or behaviors
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a knowledge file that gets injected into the AI's context during analysis. Use this when a user wants to teach the AI about environment-specific patterns, known issues, or security rules. Knowledge changes WHAT the AI knows — facts and patterns it should consider. Categories: 'wisdom' (always applied — expert tips), 'known-issues' (always applied — vendor bugs/OS quirks), 'security' (conditional — only when suspicious packets detected).

category.description: Knowledge category: 'wisdom' (always applied), 'known-issues' (always applied), 'security' (conditional on capture signals)

content.description: The markdown content of the knowledge file. Use clear headings, bullet points, and specific guidance the AI should follow.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Load a network capture to get started.
[Import Capture File](command:nettrace.importCapture)
Drag .pcap/.pcapng files into the workspace, or click above to import.

Once loaded, click the 🔍 icon on a capture to analyze it with AI.

**Agents** change HOW the AI thinks — each has a persona, prompt, tools, and filters.
[Create Agent](command:nettrace.createAgent)

Create an agent for a new analysis workflow (e.g., TLS specialist, DNS troubleshooter).
Agents filter traffic, prioritize specific anomalies, and guide the AI's approach.

💡 Tip: Ask @nettrace to create an agent for you by describing what you need.

**Knowledge** changes WHAT the AI knows — facts, rules, and patterns from your environment.
[Add Knowledge File](command:nettrace.createKnowledge)

• **Analysis Guidance** — Always applied. Expert tips, false positive avoidance.
• **Known Issues** — Always applied. Vendor bugs, OS behaviors, firewall quirks.
• **Security Heuristics** — Conditional. Activated only when capture has suspicious signals.

💡 Tip: Ask @nettrace to create knowledge files based on your expertise.

Optional: Add context about your scenario for better AI analysis.
[Edit Scenario Context](command:nettrace.editScenarioDetails)
Describe the symptoms, IPs, and topology to help the AI provide more targeted diagnosis.

nettrace.tsharkPath.description: Path to tshark executable. Leave empty to auto-detect from PATH or standard install locations.

nettrace.wiresharkPath.description: Path to Wireshark executable for 'Open in Wireshark' feature.

nettrace.defaultAgent.description: Default analysis agent to use when none is specified.

nettrace.autoParseOnAdd.description: Automatically parse capture files when added to the workspace.

nettrace.maxPacketsPerStream.description: Maximum number of packets to include per stream in LLM context.

nettrace.excludeProtocols.description: Protocols to exclude from analysis by default.
